rxn_class_summary: Gd(III)/chiral PyBox-catalyzed visible-light-promoted asymmetric [4+2] dearomative cycloaddition of 1-(2-carbonylpyridine)-substituted naphthalenes with styrenes

key_intermediates.name: triplet 1,4-biradical from Si-face addition of styrene to triplet naphthalene (III)

electron_flow: Gd(III)/PyBox coordinates the 2-carbonylpyridine-directed naphthalene to lower its excitation energy; blue-light excitation of this complex gives a singlet that undergoes ISC to a triplet naphthalene biradical, which adds enantioselectively to styrene to form a triplet 1,4-biradical, then a second ISC and radical recombination close the [4+2] ring.

probe_reagents.name: O2 (triplet quencher)
probe_reagents.observation: yield dropped to 26% (ee retained 96%), consistent with triplet intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 2,5-dimethylhexa-2,4-diene (10)
probe_reagents.observation: dose-dependent inhibition (5% yield at 100 mol%); supports triplet pathway

probe_reagents.name: benzil (triplet sensitizer, ET ~54 kcal/mol)
probe_reagents.observation: racemic 3aa in low yield (17%), poor dr/rr — direct triplet sensitization does not reproduce selective reaction

probe_reagents.name: [Ir(dFCF3ppy)2(dtbbpy)]PF6 (ET 61.8 kcal/mol)
probe_reagents.observation: 54% yield, racemic, low selectivity — rules out simple energy transfer mechanism

probe_reagents.name: (E)- vs (Z)-β-methylstyrene (2ac)
probe_reagents.observation: (E)-2ac gives 3aac (3:1 dr, 95% ee) with recovered 2ac >20:1 E/Z; (Z)-2ac gives 3aac' (1.4:1 dr, 60% ee) with recovered 2ac 0.8:1 E/Z — partial Z→E isomerization indicates reversible radical addition through a biradical, not energy transfer

notes: Job's plot established 1:1 Gd:1a binding stoichiometry; UV-vis shows a bathochromic shift (ε = 59 M^-1 cm^-1 at 448 nm) for the Gd(III)/L4/1a complex enabling visible-light absorption that the free naphthalene lacks. A linear nonlinear-effect plot (ee of 3aa vs ee of L4) implies one ligand per enantiodetermining TS. An alternative energy-transfer mechanism via free triplet naphthalene was excluded because external triplet sensitizers gave racemic, low-selectivity product and Z/E isomerization of styrene argues for reversible biradical addition rather than triplet energy transfer. Control experiments confirmed Gd(OTf)3, L4, and light are all essential.
